rulings.Case Title: HARRY BROWNSON et al VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 4, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Moore Dry Dock Company¿s motion for summary judgment or, alternatively, summary adjudication: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIE F BROWN VS. ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 4, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Albay Construction Company¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. On the issue of causation, defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specifically, plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether the products, materials and/or debris to which Albay¿s employees exposed him contained asbestos. The court will point out that on the issue of duty defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff¿s objections to the deposition of Gary Miles and to defendant¿s General Order 129 interrogatory responses are sustained. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. Defendant¿s objections to Dr. Compton¿s declaration are overruled as to paragraph 18 and sustained as to paragraphs 19, 20, 21. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIE F BROWN VS. ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 4, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Milwaukee Valve Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiff¿s claims. Defendant did not properly make an issue whether any original component part removed from a Milwaukee valve contained asbestos. Plaintiff¿s objections to Mr. Ocacio¿s deposition testimony are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIE F BROWN VS. ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 4, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. On the issue of causation, defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specifically, plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether the products, materials and/or debris to which defendant¿s employees exposed him contained asbestos. The court will point out that it did not grant the motion on the issues of workers compensation exclusivity and duty. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. Defendant¿s objections to the statements and opinions in Dr. Compton¿s declaration regarding asbestos content of the gaskets and packing at issue are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HAROLD KOEPKE et al VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 4, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Volkswagen Group of America, Inc.¿s motion to strike is denied. The complaint sufficiently pleads causes of action for fraud/failure to warn and conspiracy to defraud/failure to warn as well as a claim for punitive damages. Defendant has 10 days to answer. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HAROLD KOEPKE et al VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 4, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Volkswagen Group of America, Inc.¿s special demurrer to plaintiffs¿ fourth and sixth causes of action is overruled. The complaint sufficiently pleads causes of action for fraud/failure to warn and conspiracy to defraud/failure to warn. The court denies defendant¿s request for judicial notice Exhibits A and B. The court denies defendant¿s reply request for judicial notice Exhibits C, D, E and F. Defendant has 10 days to answer. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
